task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or Gretl script window is open, showing the creation of a script file (e.g., with a .inp extension or in the Gretl script editor).
The script contains a command to open the dataset (e.g., 'open food.gdt' or 'open /home/ga/Documents/gretl_data/food.gdt'), or the dataset is visible in the Gretl main window.

task_completion.description: Log-transform the food expenditure and income variables
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script contains commands to create log variables (e.g., 'logs food_exp income' or 'genr l_food_exp = log(food_exp)'), or log-transformed variables appear in the Gretl variable list/output.

task_completion.description: Estimate a log-log OLS regression model
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script contains an 'ols' command using the log-transformed variables. The resulting regression output should be visible, showing an income elasticity coefficient of approximately 0.6488.

task_completion.description: Perform a hypothesis test for unitary income elasticity (H0: beta1 = 1)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script contains a 'restrict' block or similar command testing if the coefficient for log-income equals 1. The output should show the restriction test results (t-statistic around -9.58, p-value < 0.05).

task_completion.description: Save the complete output to the specified text file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The script uses output redirection (e.g., 'outfile' command) to save results to '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/elasticity_test_results.txt', or a terminal/file manager shows this file has been created with the correct contents.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a Gretl script (hansl) rather than just GUI point-and-click
integrity.visual_evidence: The results should be generated by executing a script file or running code from the Gretl script editor, as explicitly required by the task, rather than manually clicking through Gretl's menus to run the regression and test.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not manually type the expected statistical results
integrity.visual_evidence: The output file 'elasticity_test_results.txt' must be generated by Gretl's output saving mechanisms. The agent should not be seen typing the elasticity value (0.6488) or p-values directly into a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that Gretl was used for the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The Gretl application or Gretl CLI (gretlcli) should be visible in the screenshots. The agent must not use Python, R, or other statistical software to bypass using Gretl.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dataset path: /home/ga/Documents/gretl_data/food.gdt. Expected output path: /home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/elasticity_test_results.txt. The log-log OLS regression of food expenditure on income should yield an income elasticity (coefficient for log-income) of approximately 0.6488 (acceptable range: 0.55 to 0.85). The hypothesis test for H0: beta1 = 1 should be strongly rejected with a p-value < 0.05 (specifically, t ≈ -9.58). The regression model explains approximately 89.2% of the variance (R-squared).
